speaker: Krishna
sanskrit: klaibyaṁ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayy upapadyate, kṣudraṁ hṛdaya-daurbalyaṁ tyaktvottiṣṭha parantapa
translation: Don't give in to this weakness, Arjuna — it doesn't suit you. Shake off this small-hearted faintness and stand up.

speaker: Krishna
translation: Heat and cold, pleasure and pain arise from sense-contact; they come and go and never last. Learn to endure them without being shaken.

speaker: Krishna
theme: no effort is wasted, starting small, motivation
translation: On this path no effort is ever lost and no step backward occurs; even a little progress on it protects one from great fear.

speaker: Krishna
theme: karma yoga, detachment from results, do the work
sanskrit: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana, mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi
translation: You have a right to your action alone, never to its fruits. Don't make results your motive — but don't fall into inaction either.

speaker: Krishna
theme: equanimity, calm in success and failure
sanskrit: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya, siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga ucyate
translation: Do your work grounded and steady, letting go of attachment, the same in success and failure. This evenness of mind is yoga.

speaker: Krishna
translation: One united in wisdom lets go of both good and bad outcomes here and now. So devote yourself to yoga — yoga is skill in action.

speaker: Krishna
theme: how anger and ruin begin, distraction, desire
translation: Dwelling on things breeds attachment; attachment breeds craving; craving breeds anger. Anger clouds judgment, clouded judgment wrecks memory and reason — and then one is lost.

speaker: Krishna
translation: Do your appointed work; action is better than inaction. Even keeping your own body going requires action.

speaker: Krishna
translation: Better your own path walked imperfectly than another's path walked well. It is better to stumble on your own dharma than to shine on someone else's.

speaker: Krishna
sanskrit: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata, abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛjāmy aham
translation: Whenever righteousness declines and wrongdoing rises, Arjuna, I manifest myself.

speaker: Krishna
theme: purpose, protecting good, age after age
sanskrit: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām, dharma-saṁsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge
translation: To protect the good, to put an end to wrongdoing, and to reestablish dharma, I appear in age after age.

speaker: Krishna
theme: self-reliance, lift yourself, be your own friend
sanskrit: uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet, ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ
translation: Lift yourself by your own effort; never drag yourself down. You alone are your own friend, and you alone are your own enemy.

speaker: Krishna
theme: mind mastery, self as friend or enemy
translation: For one who has mastered themselves, the self is a friend; but for one who hasn't, the self stays hostile, like an enemy.

speaker: Krishna
theme: surrender, letting go of worry, trust
sanskrit: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja, ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ
translation: Let go of all other obligations and take refuge in me alone. I will free you from all wrongs — do not grieve.
